div.p: Step-by-step state machine for function invocation

ol.li: Return address saved BEFORE popping args
Args popped reverse → map left-to-right to locals
Locals initialized: args first, then 0.0 defaults

div.p: IP = 6 (pointing at CALL opcode) Stack: [3.0, 5.0, 7.0] ↑ stack_top = 3 Frames: [main] locals_top = 0
IP = 13 (past CALL instruction) Operands read: arg_count = 3 entry_point = 50 locals_count = 5 Stack: [3.0, 5.0, 7.0] (unchanged)
✓ frames.count (1) < FRAMES_MAX (256) ✓ locals_top (0) + 5 <= LOCALS_MAX (65536) ✓ arg_count (3) <= locals_count (5) ✓ entry_point (50) < bytecode.count
All pass → continue Any fail → INTERPRET_RUNTIME_ERROR
return_address = IP = 13 (Points to instruction AFTER CALL)
args[2] = vm_pop() → 7.0 args[1] = vm_pop() → 5.0 args[0] = vm_pop() → 3.0 Stack: [] (empty) args = [3.0, 5.0, 7.0]
Result: args array has left-to-right order!

p.strong: Create frame at top of stack:

div.p: frame = frame_stack_push() frame->chunk = vm->chunk frame->return_address = 13 frame->locals_base = 0 frame->locals_count = 5 frames.count = 2 (main + new)

for i = 0 to 4: if i < 3: // i < arg_count locals[0+i] = args[i] else: locals[0+i] = 0.0 Result: locals[0] = 3.0 (arg0) locals[1] = 5.0 (arg1) locals[2] = 7.0 (arg2) locals[3] = 0.0 (default) locals[4] = 0.0 (default) locals_top = 5
IP = 50 (entry_point) Stack: [] Frames: [main, foo] locals: [3.0, 5.0, 7.0, 0.0, 0.0] locals_top = 5 Ready to execute function body!
foo.local_0 = locals[0] = 3.0 foo.local_1 = locals[1] = 5.0 foo.local_2 = locals[2] = 7.0 foo.local_3 = locals[3] = 0.0 foo.local_4 = locals[4] = 0.0
